Empires & Puzzles Kushanku Review: The Fist of Deception

Don't let the furry face fool you. Kushanku brings one of the most annoying control abilities in the game at breakneck speed. But in a world of stat monsters, can this Ninja still dodge his way to victory? We break down the Tumbling Dragon's kit.
Kushanku: The Raccoon Who Silences Gods
Kushanku, The Tumbling Trickster: Very Fast Control in a Furry Package

Kushanku is a 5-star Holy Paladin from the Ninja Tower event, originally released in May 2023. In an era of gods, dragons, and cosmic horrors, Kushanku stands out as... well, a raccoon with nunchucks. But don't let the cute exterior deceive you; his kit is designed to frustrate opponents with high-speed control and elusive maneuvering. He operates on the Ninja "Charge" mechanic, but unlike his damage-dealing brethren who often wait for the big x3 hit, Kushanku is almost exclusively a "fire-at-will" disruptor.

The artwork features a determined raccoon (likely a tanuki, given the Japanese ninja theme) clad in yellow robes, wielding nunchucks. It's a fantastic design that balances the whimsy of an animal hero with the serious "martial artist" vibe of the Ninja family. Compared to the dark and edgy designs of many recent heroes, Kushanku brings a bit of character to the roster. He looks like he's about to lecture you on inner peace right before hitting you in the shin.
Looking at his base stats in the context of the 2026 meta, Kushanku shows his age.
This is now considered quite low for a 5-star hero. Even with Limit Breaks, he won't be your primary damage dealer.

These were tank-worthy stats in 2023, but today they are merely "decent." He relies heavily on his Paladin class and damage reduction mechanics to survive against modern nukers who can easily hit for 1200+ damage.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family is notorious for being annoying on defense, though less reliable on offense.
chance is low (5% for 2 heroes, 10% for 3), but when it procs, it can swing a match. It's pure RNG, but dodging a sniper shot and slapping them back is always satisfying.
Kushanku relies purely on the family bonus; he does not have modern "Core" passives like start-of-battle mana or ailment immunity (outside of his LB2 power).
for the first 6 turns. This is actually excellent for him. It ensures he doesn't get silenced or mana-slowed before he can fire his first 1x Charge. It guarantees he gets to do his job as an opener.

Upon release, Kushanku was compared favorably to Malosi. He offered a way to shut down dangerous tanks or flankers at Very Fast speed with the added utility of Mindless Attack (which drains mana) rather than just blocking effects.
His stats have fallen behind significantly. The damage from his special skill is now negligible against modern health pools of 3000+. However,
. Stopping a game-ending special skill (like a modern OP hero) for 4 tiles is still a top-tier ability.

He is no longer a meta defender, but on offense, he is a surgical tool. In Rush Wars or Tournaments, he is excellent. In normal raids, he requires careful play to keep alive, but his ability to silence a key threat instantly keeps him relevant.
Note: Kushanku does not currently have a costume.

Very Fast Mindless Attack is devastating here, although his charge mechanic behaves differently in Rush (all speeds set to Very Fast usually means just one charge level, but for Ninjas, it allows rapid firing of x1).
A team dedicated to mana control and blocking specials at very fast speeds. You pick apart the enemy defense one by one while they can't cast.
Kushanku + Rhys + High Damage Dealers (e.g., Jove/Anne).
Rhys boosts tiles, helping Kushanku charge even faster. Kushanku locks down the key threat (like an enemy Ludwig or Aramis) while your heavy hitters charge up.
If a Taunt hero fires, Kushanku is forced to target them (unless he charges x1 before they fire, which he should).
They can resist the Mindless Attack status. Hitting a Cleric with Kushanku is a gamble.
Do not put him at Tank. If he hides, the enemy ghosts tiles through the center, charging their whole team.

If you pull Kushanku, level him! A hero who can stop an enemy special for just a few tiles of matching is invaluable for clearing map stages and winning raids against stronger teams. Don't worry about his damage; use him to stop the boss from killing your team.

He is a luxury tool. He doesn't need Alpha Aethers or heavy investment to do his job (stopping a skill). If you have spare Paladin emblems, he's a great depth piece for War attacks, specifically for countering slow, devastating heroes like Alfrike or Rush War tanks. Don't prioritize him over modern stat monsters, but don't feed him away.
Originally, the community was lukewarm on him, but a buff moving the
Mindless Attack to the x1 Charge
- A common reaction to his buff.
"I pulled the raccoon... I think it’s actually pretty weak."
- Some players were disappointed by his low damage output compared to nukers like Jove.
"He is not a killer... hes the one that might give your hitters the time to wreck havoc."
- The wise take. He buys time.
Many players debated if he replaced Malosi. The consensus: Kushanku is generally better for the mana drain, but Malosi is safer against passive trigger heroes.

Kushanku is the definition of a "utility" hero. He won't appear on the top of the damage charts, and he won't be the last man standing in a 1v5 clutch. But he is the reason your team survived long enough to win. In a game dominated by massive damage, sometimes the strongest move is simply telling the opponent, "No."
Keep him, emblem him for survival, and enjoy the frustration on your opponent's face when their full-mana hero hits their own ally.
